objective: Win the crowd and secure a paid christening on Patent Row
opposition: Skeptical inventors and a crowd already numb to gimmicks
turn: Silas improvises a filthy, perfect product name and flips ridicule into applause
consequence_cost: His local celebrity hardens into expectation that he can name anything

objective: Collect payment and get offstage before anyone asks for more
opposition: A spill-born glow-moss crawls over a signboard and the crowd demands another miracle
turn: Silas jokingly calls the bloom bridelight and the crowd repeats it
consequence_cost: The throwaway joke becomes attached to a living phenomenon

objective: Dismiss the moss as good copy and go home
opposition: Damaris and Gideon close in while more onlookers test the new word
turn: By dusk the same pink glow appears on other walls across Patent Row
consequence_cost: Silas realizes his joke has entered the city's ecology and cannot be recalled

objective: Brush off the bridelight fuss as publicity
opposition: Inez intercepts Silas with damaged lamp trays and furious household evidence
turn: Neighbors chant a different name and a live patch turns blue in front of him
consequence_cost: He can no longer deny that public speech is changing working light

objective: Protect his reputation by staying glib
opposition: Inez walks him through homes where the shifting moss is affecting heat and safety
turn: A child burns two fingers when a lamp output changes mid-task
consequence_cost: Silas becomes morally implicated in local harm and newly alert to Inez

objective: Arrive at Junie's kitchen looking solvent and in control
opposition: Junie has his unpaid meals, borrowed coins, and rising rent figures ready
turn: She shows that neighborhood prestige around the glow has already raised nearby costs
consequence_cost: Silas's money trouble becomes inseparable from the moss craze

objective: Eat a real meal and pretend the city is still ordinary
opposition: Otis keeps repeating new names and Junie refuses to indulge Silas's vanity
turn: Silas hears how quickly street language becomes domestic speech at the table
consequence_cost: His talent stops feeling purely theatrical

objective: See what Damaris wants without losing autonomy
opposition: Damaris wants serialized spectacle and warns Gideon will buy him first
turn: Her offer makes clear that silence will not keep him outside the story
consequence_cost: Silas recognizes that he himself is now being marketed

objective: Get enough money to cover pigment debt and buy time
opposition: Gideon's retainer requires exclusivity over the runaway moss's stable public name
turn: Silas signs the exploratory contract despite knowing the terms smell wrong
consequence_cost: He is formally tied to Gideon's campaign and can no longer claim neutrality

objective: Learn the moss well enough to solve it neatly
opposition: Sump Ward's layered uses and distrust resist any single elegant explanation
turn: Households demonstrate different names tied to different practical results
consequence_cost: Silas accepts that local naming is infrastructure and agrees to work with Inez

objective: Launch Celadon Crown as the stable official name
opposition: Mixed crowds arrive ready to heckle, test, and contradict
turn: Gideon's early stagecraft briefly seems to be working
consequence_cost: The campaign appears salvageable long enough to raise the fall

objective: Fix the name in public memory by painting it huge on a wet wall
opposition: Balcony voices and street kids answer with local names as soon as the chant begins
turn: The moss shifts colors faster than the paint can dry
consequence_cost: The official unveiling becomes visibly unstable in front of buyers

objective: Save dignity and keep the contract alive
opposition: Spattering pigment ruins clothing and the crowd breaks into laughter and argument
turn: Damaris seizes the fiasco for tomorrow's front page
consequence_cost: Silas's authority becomes public comedy and the naming fight goes citywide

objective: Clear contaminated slime from Junie's kitchen before dinner service
opposition: Sticky spores, Junie's anger, and Silas's shame make the work slow
turn: Inez arrives because she knows how to save the lamp trays
consequence_cost: Forced practical cooperation replaces public posturing

objective: Finish the cleanup without exposing fresh weakness
opposition: Shared labor, hot food, and Junie's refusal to let anyone grandstand strip their defenses
turn: Inez explains what names mean in kitchens and crossing ways while Silas actually listens
consequence_cost: Respect and desire emerge in a quiet space neither of them controls

objective: Convince himself he can take Gideon's money without becoming a thief
opposition: Gideon plainly wants neighborhood names harvested and polished for sale
turn: Silas accepts because the advance clears his immediate debt
consequence_cost: He chooses complicity before the theft is fully enacted

objective: Collect names as harmless research
opposition: People in Ferrymouth and Patent Row feed him jokes, decoys, and misdirection
turn: He realizes fake names are a form of community self-defense
consequence_cost: His task becomes more predatory because sincerity will not be given

objective: Get something real from Sump Ward without asking directly
opposition: Inez has frozen him out and Aunt Vero watches him like a trapdoor
turn: He overhears a working exchange and memorizes a private name anyway
consequence_cost: He crosses the moral line from listener to thief

objective: Satisfy Gideon with as little damage as possible
opposition: Gideon wants everything useful and moves fast once he has it
turn: Overnight labels appear using stolen ward language
consequence_cost: Silas can no longer pretend the harm is hypothetical

objective: Blend into the Feast of Mud Names and maybe begin repairing what he broke
opposition: The feast crowd notices the new labels match ward phrases and Inez identifies the source
turn: Public accusation turns into a trough dunking that wrenches Silas's wrist
consequence_cost: Silas is humiliated, injured, and effectively barred from Sump Ward

objective: Return to ordinary paid work and stop drowning in shame
opposition: His injured wrist will not hold a clean line and clients do not wait
turn: He cannot paint well enough to earn the way he usually does
consequence_cost: His income collapses at the same moment his public standing does

objective: Make himself useful at Junie's kitchen instead of uselessly apologizing
opposition: Lunch rush, fuel strain, and Junie's impatience leave no room for theatrics
turn: Otis repeats a branded schoolyard slogan and Junie goes cold with anger
consequence_cost: Silas sees the campaign entering family speech as contamination

objective: Figure out what his disgrace means beyond his own ego
opposition: Damaris insists the city is treating the fight like entertainment until he looks harder
turn: From the ferry he watches local signs disappear under Celadon repainting
consequence_cost: He understands naming as dispossession rather than bad publicity

objective: Offer Inez labor and truth instead of another performance
opposition: Brutal salvage work and Inez's distrust keep him at a distance
turn: Silas admits the contract, the money, and the weakness without polishing the story
consequence_cost: A narrow corridor of trust opens because honesty replaces theater

objective: Hold that truce through the long night
opposition: Heat, exhaustion, bodily proximity, and unsorted desire make distance impossible
turn: They have sex in the propagation shed and afterward Inez hands him her private ledger
consequence_cost: Their bond becomes erotic and strategic, raising the cost of any future failure

objective: Use the ledger to understand the runaway strain
opposition: The entries point to old names and uses but not yet to origin
turn: Cross-references reveal the moss descends from a preexisting communal strain
consequence_cost: The problem acquires a buried history

objective: Confirm that history with someone who was there
opposition: Aunt Vero resents both exploitation and neat retrospective heroics
turn: She explains how ward women bred the ancestor strain and hid it from speculators
consequence_cost: The conflict becomes theft of communal labor, not just brand abuse

objective: See what Gideon is planning before he launches it
opposition: High Ward access is controlled and the preview is meant for buyers, not enemies
turn: Damaris slips Silas in long enough for him to understand the regatta's saturation design
consequence_cost: He learns the official name will be driven through song, print, and free cuttings

objective: Choose the next move with Inez
opposition: Exposure alone would still leave someone claiming sole ownership
turn: They agree to make monopoly impossible rather than proving a single rightful owner
consequence_cost: The strategy shifts from debunking to plural public use

objective: Get hostile neighbors and allies into the same room long enough to talk
opposition: Too many people distrust Silas or hate one another's districts
turn: Junie uses supper and plain insistence to keep everyone seated
consequence_cost: A temporary coalition becomes possible through hospitality

objective: Gather names without stealing them again
opposition: Each person insists that a name arrives with a story, a task, or a warning
turn: Silas starts recording context instead of just collecting phrases
consequence_cost: He begins learning a non-extractive relation to language

objective: Turn the supper's energy into a viable tactic
opposition: Some fear that many names at once will only deepen the chaos
turn: Aunt Vero proposes chorus boards and distributed naming across ferries, stoops, and wash lines
consequence_cost: The coalition adopts plurality as both method and ethic

objective: Appear at Gideon's regatta launch without actually giving him what he wants
opposition: Gideon has bought Junie's fuel notes and can shut her kitchen down if Silas refuses
turn: With the speaking horn in hand and no clean way out Silas publicly christens the moss Celadon Crown
consequence_cost: The official name catches and Inez walks away from him

objective: Outrun the immediate consequences of the regatta
opposition: The new name spreads through songs, posters, schools, and household chatter
turn: Lamp beds in poor districts shift to pale gold, pretty but less useful
consequence_cost: Daily life becomes harder and more expensive for the people already squeezed

objective: Save either his shop or Junie's margins from the fallout
opposition: Landlord pressure, moss overgrowth, and branded fuel prices close every escape route
turn: Silas loses the shop while Junie buys expensive branded fuel just to stay open
consequence_cost: He is stripped of livelihood and forced to face material ruin

objective: Make himself useful without asking anyone to forgive him
opposition: Ward crews do not want speeches or cleverness from him
turn: Junie assigns him to cleanup labor where only stamina matters
consequence_cost: Silas enters a form of work that gives him no prestige

objective: Endure uncredited labor long enough to learn from it
opposition: Heat, stink, and more experienced workers expose how little he knows
turn: He hears multiple precise names for the same moss tied to different tasks
consequence_cost: Plural naming becomes functionally legible to him from the inside

objective: Decide whether he still has any rightful role in the fight
opposition: The temptation to reclaim genius and centrality keeps surfacing
turn: He conceives the chorus board as a surface for other people's names, not his signature
consequence_cost: His sense of self shifts from author to facilitator

objective: Build enough chorus boards to matter before Gideon's final blessing
opposition: Time, money, and trust are all short
turn: Damaris contributes spoiled newsprint and paste because chaos can still be useful
consequence_cost: The coalition gains the materials it needs without respectability

objective: Get the boards distributed across the city before they are torn down
opposition: Gideon's runners mock the tactic and rip down early placements
turn: Otis and other children move the boards faster than adults can suppress them
consequence_cost: The plan starts propagating through the same channels Gideon relies on

objective: Interrupt the public script without making Silas the hero again
opposition: Gideon's stagecraft and Silas's old appetite for the microphone both threaten the tactic
turn: Inez takes the first board and gives the first public name tied to use rather than fashion
consequence_cost: The confrontation shifts away from Silas-centered charisma

objective: Keep plurality alive long enough for the moss to answer
opposition: Crowd confusion and Gideon's last push for a single chant could still collapse the moment
turn: Names rise from multiple wards and the bridge moss holds bands and islands of different color
consequence_cost: Exclusive branding visibly fails and public naming power redistributes

objective: End Gideon's claim over his work without sliding back into vanity
opposition: Gideon offers restored fame, royalties, and elegant rehabilitation
turn: Silas burns the contract and gives Damaris the papers showing how names were stripped for sale
consequence_cost: He forfeits proprietary success and widens the fallout beyond himself

objective: Ask Inez for a future that is not built on performance
opposition: Inez refuses absolution and insists on terms measured in work and shared credit
turn: They agree to a conditional partnership in life and signage rather than a grand romantic reset
consequence_cost: The romance resumes on honest, adult terms

objective: Live inside the new plural city without pretending it is solved
opposition: Profiteers remain, arguments continue, and no single system has replaced the old one
turn: On an autumn ferry run a fresh ridiculous name from Otis starts another small color shift
consequence_cost: Silas and Inez accept ongoing communal instability as the right price of shared life
